contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमान् शशधरश्चन्द्रो ताराधीशो निशाकरः ।
सुधानिधिः सदाराध्यः सत्पतिः साधुपूजितः ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmān śaśadharaśchandrō tārādhīśō niśākaraḥ ।
sudhānidhiḥ sadārādhyaḥ satpatiḥ sādhupūjitaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śrīmān (the auspicious), Śaśadhara (bearer of the hare), Chandra (the Moon), Tārādhīśa (lord of the stars), Niśākara (maker of night), Sudhānidhi (treasure of nectar), Sadārādhya (ever worshipped), Satpati (lord of the good), Sādhupūjita (worshipped by the saintly). (1)

contentBlocks.original:
जितेन्द्रियो जगद्योनिः ज्योतिश्चक्रप्रवर्तकः ।
विकर्तनानुजो वीरो विश्वेशो विदुषां पतिः ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jitēndriyō jagadyōniḥ jyōtiśchakrapravartakaḥ ।
vikartanānujō vīrō viśvēśō viduṣāṃ patiḥ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jitendriya (conqueror of the senses), Jagadyoni (womb of the world), Jyotiścakrapravartaka (mover of the wheel of luminaries), Vikartanānuja (younger brother of the Sun), Vīra (heroic), Viśveśa (lord of the universe), Viduṣāṃ Pati (lord of the learned). (2)

Doṣākara (maker of the night), Duṣṭadūra (far from the wicked), Puṣṭimān (nourishing), Śiṣṭapālaka (protector of the righteous), Aṣṭamūrtipriya (beloved of the eight-formed Śiva), Ananta (endless), Kaṣṭadārukuṭhāraka (axe to the tree of suffering). (3)

contentBlocks.original:
स्वप्रकाशः प्रकाशात्मा द्युचरो देवभोजनः ।
कलाधरः कालहेतुः कामकृत्कामदायकः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svaprakāśaḥ prakāśātmā dyucharō dēvabhōjanaḥ ।
kaḻādharaḥ kālahētuḥ kāmakṛtkāmadāyakaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Svaprakāśa (self-luminous), Prakāśātman (of luminous essence), Dyuchara (moving through the sky), Devabhojana (nectar, food of the gods), Kaḷādhara (holder of the lunar phases), Kālahetu (cause of time), Kāmakṛt (maker of desires), Kāmadāyaka (fulfiller of desires). (4)

Mṛtyusaṃhāraka (destroyer of death), Amartya (immortal), Nityānuṣṭhānadāyaka (giver of daily spiritual practice), Kṣapākara (maker of night), Kṣīṇapāpa (one whose sins are destroyed), Kṣayavṛddhisamanvita (accompanied by waning and waxing). (5)

contentBlocks.original:
जैवातृकः शुची शुभ्रो जयी जयफलप्रदः ।
सुधामयः सुरस्वामी भक्तनामिष्टदायकः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaivātṛkaḥ śuchī śubhrō jayī jayaphalapradaḥ ।
sudhāmayaḥ surasvāmī bhaktanāmiṣṭadāyakaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Jaivātṛka (the life-giver), Śuci (pure), Śubhra (brilliantly white), Jayī (victorious), Jayaphalaprada (giver of the fruit of victory), Sudhāmaya (made of nectar), Surasvāmī (lord of the gods), Bhaktanāmiṣṭadāyaka (fulfiller of all desires of devotees). (6)

contentBlocks.original:
भुक्तिदो मुक्तिदो भद्रो भक्तदारिद्र्यभञ्जकः ।
सामगानप्रियः सर्वरक्षकः सागरोद्भवः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhuktidō muktidō bhadrō bhaktadāridryabhañjakaḥ ।
sāmagānapriyaḥ sarvarakṣakaḥ sāgarōdbhavaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Bhuktida (giver of enjoyment), Muktida (giver of liberation), Bhadra (auspicious), Bhaktadāridryabhañjaka (destroyer of devotees' poverty), Sāmagānapriya (fond of Sāma Veda chanting), Sarvarakṣaka (protector of all), Sāgarodbhava (born of the ocean). (7)

Bhayāntakṛt (destroyer of fear), Bhaktigamya (attained through devotion), Bhavabandhavimocaka (liberator from the bonds of existence), Jagatprakāśakiraṇa (rays that illuminate the world), Jagadānandakāraṇa (cause of the world's joy). (8)

contentBlocks.original:
निस्सपत्नो निराहारो निर्विकारो निरामयः ।
भूच्छायाऽऽच्छादितो भव्यो भुवनप्रतिपालकः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nissapatnō nirāhārō nirvikārō nirāmayaḥ ।
bhūchChāyā''chChāditō bhavyō bhuvanapratipālakaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nissapatna (without rival), Nirāhāra (beyond nourishment), Nirvikāra (without modification), Nirāmaya (free from disease), Bhūchāyācchādita (veiled by the shadow of the earth in eclipse), Bhavya (auspicious), Bhuvanapratipālaka (sustainer of all the worlds). (9)

contentBlocks.original:
सकलार्तिहरः सौम्यजनकः साधुवन्दितः ।
सर्वागमज्ञः सर्वज्ञो सनकादिमुनिस्तुतः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalārtiharaḥ saumyajanakaḥ sādhuvanditaḥ ।
sarvāgamajñaḥ sarvajñō sanakādimunistutaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sakalārtihara (remover of all afflictions), Saumyajanaka (father of Mercury), Sādhuvanditaḥ (saluted by the saintly), Sarvāgamajña (knower of all sacred sciences), Sarvajña (omniscient), Sanakādimunistuta (praised by Sanaka and the other great sages). (10)

Sitacchatradhvajopeta (endowed with white parasol and banner), Sitāṅga (of white limbs), Sitabhūṣaṇa (with white ornaments), Śvetamālyāmbaradhara (wearing white garlands and robes), Śvetagandhānulepana (anointed with white sandal paste). (11)

Daśāśvarathasaṃrūḍha (mounted on a ten-horse chariot), Daṇḍapāṇi (staff in hand), Dhanurdhara (bearing a bow), Kundapuṣpojjvalākāra (resplendent as the kunda flower), Nayanābjasamudbhava (born from the lotus-eye of Viṣṇu). (12)

Ātreyagōtraja (of the lineage of the sage Atri), Atyantavinaya (exceedingly humble), Priyadāyaka (giver of joy), Karuṇārasasampūrṇa (full of the essence of compassion), Karkaṭaprabhu (lord of Cancer), Avyaya (imperishable). (13)

Chaturaśrāsanārūḍha (seated on a square throne), Catura (the clever one), Divyavāhana (of divine vehicle), Vivasvanmaṇḍalāgneyavāsa (dwelling in the fiery orb of the Sun), Vasusamṛddhida (giver of abundant wealth). (14)

contentBlocks.original:
महेश्वरप्रियो दान्तः मेरुगोत्रप्रदक्षिणः ।
ग्रहमण्डलमध्यस्थो ग्रसितार्को ग्रहाधिपः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahēśvarapriyō dāntaḥ mērugōtrapradakṣiṇaḥ ।
grahamaṇḍalamadhyasthō grasitārkō grahādhipaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Maheśvarapriya (beloved of Maheśvara), Dānta (self-controlled), Merugotrapradakṣiṇa (circumambulating the Meru range), Grahamaṇḍalamadhyastha (dwelling at the centre of the planetary orb), Grasitārka (swallower of the Sun in eclipse), Grahādhipa (lord of the planets). (15)

contentBlocks.original:
द्विजराजो द्युतिलको द्विभुजो द्विजपूजितः ।
औदुम्बरनगावास उदारो रोहिणीपतिः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvijarājō dyutilakō dvibhujō dvijapūjitaḥ ।
audumbaranagāvāsa udārō rōhiṇīpatiḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dvijarāja (king of the twice-born), Dyutilaka (the mark of light), Dvibhuja (two-armed), Dvijapūjita (worshipped by the twice-born), Audumbaranagāvāsa (dwelling on the udumbara tree), Udāra (noble), Rohiṇīpati (lord of Rohiṇī). (16)

Nityodaya (ever rising), Munistutya (praised by the sages), Nityānandaphalaprada (giver of the fruit of eternal bliss), Sakalāhlādanakara (cause of the delight of all), Palāśasamidhapriya (fond of palāśa-wood as sacred fuel). (17)
Thus [have been recited] these one hundred and eight names of the lord of the nakṣatras (the Moon).
Thus ends the Śrī Chandra Aṣṭottaraśatanāma Stotram.
